person_relationships.description: Both investigated in 2023 Ridout Road rental issue; CPIB found no wrongdoing. Coordinated on foreign affairs and national security.
Fu served as his Second Minister for Environment and Water Resources (2012-2015)
Initiated legal proceedings against Lee Hsien Yang in 2023 for defamation regarding Ridout Road rental statements

political_stances.stance_content: Called for renewing commitment to multilateralism in an era of turbulence, at the UN General Assembly 80th session.
Singapore must hold on to its ability to say no - even at a cost - or risk losing its relevance on the world stage. Uphold principles, be consistent.
Foreign policy now has domestic salience; must explain Singapore's foreign policy to Singaporeans. Foreign policy begins at home.
Warned that economic fallout from the Iran war could worsen and markets have yet to fully price in risks.
Took full responsibility for mistake in assuring the public that TraceTogether data would only be used for contact tracing when police could access it for criminal investigations.

major_achievements.achievement: Served as Medical Director of Singapore National Eye Centre and CEO of Singapore General Hospital concurrently
Appointed Minister for Foreign Affairs, representing Singapore globally for a decade
Led Singapore's Smart Nation Initiative as Minister-in-charge
Launched the book 'Not so Little Red Dot: 60 Years of Singapore's Diplomacy' marking six decades of diplomatic history

biography_summary: Vivian Balakrishnan is a Singaporean politician, diplomat and former ophthalmologist who has served as Minister for Foreign Affairs since 2015. A President's Scholar of Chindian heritage (Tamil father, Hoklo mother), he trained in medicine at NUS and ophthalmology at Moorfields Eye Hospital, London. He was Medical Director of the Singapore National Eye Centre and CEO of Singapore General Hospital before entering politics in 2001. He previously served as Minister for Community Development, Youth and Sports (2004-2011), Environment and Water Resources (2011-2015), and Minister-in-charge of Smart Nation (2014-2021). He was investigated in the 2023 Ridout Road rental matter and cleared by CPIB. He is married to Joy Chia Oon Su with four children.
